facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.fact: CSI should be treated as a top-three architectural decision, not merely a storage subsystem feature.
facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.baseline_missed_reason: The baseline overweighted API semantics and selected Server-Side Apply as rank 3. It did not generalize from CRI to the broader out-of-tree interface extraction motif.

facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.fact: FlexVolume was a real competing/preceding proposal, but CSI won because FlexVolume did not solve root filesystem access and dependency/deployment problems.
consult_lineage: consult_004_csi_response.json pressure warnings CONFLICTING_PATHWAY_REINFORCEMENT and TASK_PATHWAY_COLLISION
facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.baseline_missed_reason: The baseline did not inspect CSI competing proposals and therefore missed FlexVolume as a necessary historical contrast.

facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.fact: CSI migration preserved existing Kubernetes storage APIs while delegating operations to CSI drivers, which explains why the architecture change can be hidden from users.
facts_recovered_only_after_consultation.baseline_missed_reason: The baseline interpreted importance through visible API machinery changes; CSI migration was less visible because user-facing PV/PVC/StorageClass APIs were intentionally preserved.
